channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Democrats to elect new leader as party struggles to repair brand
item.description: Democrats are poised to elect a new leader in a low-profile Democratic National Committee election that could have big implications for the party’s future.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Two front-runners remain in DNC chair race
item.description: The two front-runners in the chair's race are Ken Martin, chair of the Minnesota Democratic-Farmer-Labor Party, and Ben Wikler, chair of the Wisconsin Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Political parties explain Hillsborough County's conservative flip
item.description: As of Thursday, Hillsborough County records showed the number of active registered Republicans was bigger than the number of registered Democrats. The difference as of Friday was 307 voters.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Colombian president urges illegal immigrants in US to return home days after diplomatic spat
item.description: The president of Colombia is urging his nationals in the U.S. illegally to return to Colombia, days after a diplomatic spat with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Legal experts say Kash Patel's opposition to warrant requirement is not a major split
item.description: Legal experts say that while FBI director nominee Kash Patel's opposition to a warrant requirement shocked some lawmakers, the warrant requirement debate is far from new.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration fires prosecutors involved in Jan. 6 cases and moves toward ousting FBI agents
item.description: The Trump administration has fired a group of prosecutors involved in the Jan. 6 criminal cases and demanded the names of FBI agents involved in those same probes so that they can possibly be ousted.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The DNC's outgoing chair says Democrats should have stuck with Joe Biden in 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the Democratic National Committee prepares to elect a new chair, its departing leader says Democrats should have stuck with Joe Biden in the 2024 race.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US aid agency is in upheaval during foreign assistance freeze and staff departures
item.description: Trump administration changes have upended the U.S. agency charged with providing humanitarian aid overseas, with senior officials put on leave, contractors laid off and a sweeping freeze imposed on foreign assistance.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's FBI chief pick, Kash Patel, insists he has no 'enemies list' and won't seek retribution
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kash Patel, President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the FBI, insisted to deeply skeptical Democrats on Thursday that he did not have an “enemies list" and that the bureau under his leadership would not seek retribution against the president's adversaries or launch investigations for
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Doug Burgum as interior secretary after Trump tasked him to boost drilling
item.description: The Senate confirmed Doug Burgum as interior secretary late Thursday after President Donald Trump tapped the North Dakota billionaire to spearhead the Republican administration's ambitions to boost fossil fuel production. The vote was 79-18.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans advance Trump OMB nominee as Democrats boycott vote
item.description: Senate Republicans advanced the nomination of Russell Vought to lead the White House budget office as Democrats boycotted the meeting to protest the administration's recent efforts to freeze large swaths of federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio says Trump’s interest in Greenland and Panama Canal is legit and driven by China concerns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio says President Donald Trump’s desire to acquire Greenland and retake control of the Panama Canal is driven by legitimate national security interests stemming from growing concerns about Chinese activity and influence in the Arctic and in Latin America
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican border cities can expect higher unemployment, bankruptcies with Trump tariffs, Baja business leaders say
item.description: According to Vega Álvarez, consumers on both sides of the border will be the ones who will pay for the tariffs as "things will have to be overpriced.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: DEI drops and House drama won't stop Black Entrepreneurs Day at the Capitol
item.description: Activist Sheletta Brundidge says the third annual Black Entrepreneurs Day, which is scheduled for Monday, will go off without a hitch, despite being unexpectedly short of its $25,000 price tag.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: President Trump casts blame during briefing on deadly plane crash
item.description: President Donald Trump started Thursday’s press briefing with a moment of silence for the victims and their families after a deadly crash involving an American Airlines plane and an Army helicopter near Ronald Reagan National Airport on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump blames diversity hiring as probe into deadly D.C. plane crash begins
item.description: As the nation reeled from the deadliest American aviation disaster in more than two decades, President Donald Trump on Thursday baselessly blamed diversity initiatives for undermining air safety and questioned the actions of a U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Democrats leave 'emergency' meeting vowing ‘street fight’ in response to Trump actions
item.description: House Democrats launched a multilateral effort Wednesday to combat the flood of executive actions from President Trump, vowing to punch back through legislation, lawsuits and a blitz of counter-messaging.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right health recommendations
item.description: The latest KFF survey finds 42 percent of respondents say they have either a great deal or fair amount of trust in Trump to make the right recommendations when it comes to health issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: White House budget office rescinds federal funding freeze memo
item.description: The Trump administration’s Office of Management and Budget released a new memo Wednesday rescinding a controversial memo issued late Monday that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance, which had paralyzed many federal programs and caused a huge uproar on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Center for Victims of Torture dramatically slashes workforce after Trump administration orders
item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive orders forced many international humanitarian organizations, like St. Paul-based Center for Victims of Torture, to cut spending and furlough staff.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr., Trump's health secretary pick, grilled about vaccines and abortion
item.description: Senators grilled Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on vaccines, abortion and Medicaid in his confirmation hearing to lead HHS. RFK Jr. has another hearing Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin and Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: Former Congresswoman Shelley Berkley settles in her new role as Las Vegas mayor
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Mayor Shelley Berkley is settling into her new role after decades of Goodman leadership in Las Vegas. The former congresswoman now holds the title of mayor.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump admin's freeze on federal grants, loans
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked a Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans that could total trillions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's Nevada voters united on top issues while Harris backers couldn't, NVPOP poll shows
item.description: A new poll that examines the driving forces behind Nevada voters' decisions in the 2024 election shows that President Donald Trump's support came from people who were focused on two primary issues: the economy and immigration.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signs order that likely sets in motion a future ban on transgender troops
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday directing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to revise the Pentagon’s policy on transgender troops, likely setting in motion a future ban on their military service.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida House and Senate leaders take charge in special session showdown
item.description: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis pushed for a special session to help boost President Donald Trump's immigration efforts. But that session was blocked by Senate and House leadership Monday.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump addresses House Republicans at Florida retreat: 5 takeaways
item.description: DORAL, Fla. — President Trump floated running for a third term, commented on a new Chinese artificial intelligence app, and urged House Republicans to unify and quickly advance his legislative priorities in a speech to the lawmakers at their annual policy retreat.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Florida legislative leaders respond to DeSantis attacks, imply he didn't read TRUMP Act or is lying
item.description: Florida's congressional leaders fired back at Gov. Ron DeSantis after he accused of them of "theatrics" Monday.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5
